title: Integer Underflow in finalizeBridgeERC20 Allows Permanent DoS of Withdrawals
reason: Not reportable as described. In Solidity 0.8 the subtraction reverts atomically, so no underflowed state is stored, and the messenger marks the relay as failed/replayable rather than permanently breaking future withdrawals.

title: ETH Can Be Permanently Locked in finalizeBridgeETH Due to Failed Transfer
reason: This is expected bridge behavior, not a contract flaw. A failed ETH delivery causes the cross-domain message to fail and remain replayable; permanent lockup only occurs when the chosen recipient always rejects ETH, which is documented and recipient-driven.

title: No Validation That Deposits Sufficiently Cover Withdrawal in finalizeBridgeERC20
reason: Duplicate of the rejected underflow theory. Insufficient deposits cause a normal revert with full state rollback, not a persistent corruption or pair-wide permanent halt by themselves.

title: initialize() Function Lacks Access Control Allowing Potential Front-Running
reason: Subsumed by F-001. The real issue is stronger: `initialize()` can be called repeatedly at any time because `clearLegacySlot` resets the initializer state.

title: Gas Estimation in CrossDomainMessenger May Cause Message Relay Failures
reason: Documented UX footgun rather than a protocol vulnerability. Failed relays are an intended replayable state in this messenger design.

title: OptimismMintableERC20 Allows Zero Address for Bridge and Remote Token
reason: This is deployer misconfiguration, not an exploitable protocol issue in the audited code.

title: onlyEOA Modifier Bypass Via Recently Created EOAs
reason: Non-reportable. The code explicitly documents that `onlyEOA` is only a best-effort UX guard and is not relied on for security.
